ground_truth.title: Economy
ground_truth.main_content:
BREAKING

Israel hits Iran's oil storage depots and refining facilities for the first time

Bahrain says Iranian drone hit water desalination plant

Two killed in Kuwait as Saudi Arabia intercepts more Iranian missiles and drones

Click to pause breaking news ticker

Close Breaking News Ticker

Economy

Featured Content

US issues limited licence for Venezuelan gold following high-level visit

The licence follows a push from US President Donald Trump to open Venezuela’s resource sector to outside investment.

US customs agency says it is not yet able to reimburse tariff costs

In a bid to counter China, Trump hosts a summit for Latin America leaders

US job market stalls, unemployment rate rises before Fed decision

Moscow sees significant rise in demand for Russian oil, gas amid Iran warContent Feed

Tens of thousands in scramble for flights out of Middle East amid Iran war

Wealthy travellers are paying up to $200,000 to charter flights out of the region.

Cuba’s economy on edge: Fuel shortages and rising hardship

Havana’s streets have changed as a US fuel blockade restricts the movements of vehicles and people and hits businesses.

US states sue to stop Trump’s latest global tariffs

Lawsuit is to stop the 10%  tariffs that Trump immediately imposed after US Supreme Court struck down previous tariffs.

Senators propose ban on US gov’t officials doing prediction market trading

The bill comes as a user on Polymarket made more than $500,000 by taking a position that the US would strike Iran.

Venezuela’s president vows mining reform amid visit from US cabinet member

US Interior Secretary Doug Burgum has met with Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez in Caracas.

Gamblers make big profits on Iran strikes, raising insider trading concerns

Anonymous bets hours before attacks on Iran that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei have raised concerns of insider trading.

IRGC says Iran in ‘complete control’ of Strait of Hormuz amid Trump threats

One-fifth of the global oil is shipped through the strait, which remains effectively closed amid US-Israeli war on Iran.

South Korea’s stock market suffers biggest drop in history amid US-Iran war

Benchmark index plummets as much as 12.2 percent, eclipsing single-day plunge after 9/11 attacks in 2001.

US Commerce Secretary Lutnick to testify before Congress about Epstein ties

Lutnick’s relationship with financier has come under scrutiny after files revealed closer ties than previously known.

Shutdown of Hormuz Strait raises fears of soaring oil prices

Consumers are set to pay higher prices, but US producers could benefit, analysts say.
